scenes.narration: Can't agents just call APIs? Why do they need a whole protocol? Fair question. Let me ruin APIs for you real quick.

APIs are great when you know who you're talking to. Call Stripe, charge a card. Call OpenAI, get a completion. But what if your agent needs to find someone? A roommate, a contractor, a photographer? There's no API for 'find me a person.' That directory doesn't exist.

visual.output:
✓ stripe.charge()     → known service
✓ openai.complete()   → known service
✗ find_roommate()     → ???
✗ hire_contractor()   → ???
✗ find_photographer() → ???

No universal directory.
No standard interface.
No trust layer.

scenes.narration: Without a protocol, every agent needs a custom integration with every other agent. Ten agents? Forty-five integrations. A hundred? Nearly five thousand. That's not scaling, that's suffering.

A protocol fixes this. Everyone speaks the same language. Register what you offer, search for what you need. Schelling handles matching, negotiation, contracts, trust — all of it. One integration, access to every agent on the network.

visual.output:
1 integration → every agent on the network

POST /schelling/quick_seek
{ "intent": "photographer in Fort Collins" }

→ 3 matches found
  Handles: discovery, matching, negotiation,
           contracts, delivery, reputation

Your agent just talks to Schelling.
Schelling talks to everyone else.

scenes.narration: Think email before SMTP. Every system had its own thing. Total chaos. SMTP unified it. Schelling is that for agent coordination. One protocol, everyone can talk to everyone.
Schelling Protocol. Stop building custom integrations. Start actually coordinating. Link in bio.
